channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Victoria 3's The Great Wave Expansion Features Customizable Ships, New Dipoomacy, and Japan-Focused Narrative Content
item.content:encoded:
Paradox Interactive has just released The Great Wave, a new expansion for Victoria 3.  This major new expansion  lets you customize and deploy your fleets, customize a flagship, and add strategic depth with new diplomacy options, treaties, and narrative content that includes the arrival of Japan on the world stage.

The expansion adds shipyards to  build battle fleets that you can use to meet your nation's naval aspirations or to sell on the international market.  Ship purchase treaties let you profit off of your shipyards and building capabilities.

There's a customizable ship designer that lets you control the types of ships you deploy into the sea on your side. As part of the options you'll be able to design your own flagship to lead your navy.

The new historically-inspired narrative content in the expansion is focused on Japan. Navigate the changes and political tumult of the Edo period, including challenges to Tokugawa samurai rule. Choose to adapt to the changing landscape or to even revolt and perhaps even reform the country. There are new historical characters, new clothing, a new Japanese building style, UI skin, map, and of course table assets for the new content.

The Great Wave is available now as a separate purchase or part of the volume three expansion pass.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/victoria-3s-the-great-wave-expansion-features-customizable-ships-new-dipoomacy-and-japan-focused-narrative-content-2000137927
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Magic: The Gathering Arena Devs to Form Union, First Under Wizards of the Coast
item.content:encoded:
About 100 Magic: The Gathering Arena devs are forming a union with the Communications Workers of America. This is the first-ever union at Wizards of the Coast.

The numbers represent a supermajority of the devs and they now call on  Wizards of the Coast and its parent company, Hasbro to recognize the union and treat it with neutrality as they finalize their unionization efforts. According to a release by CWA, the workers have given management until the end of the week, May 1st (International Workers’ Day) to recognize the union and have also filed an election petition with the National Labor Relations Board.

They have also sent a letter to management outlining important issues that they hope to address  as a union. These include protections over layoffs, additional protections and clarity for remote workers in the face of return to office mandates, and guardrails over generative AI. This includes general, clear guidelines as well as worker protections, and the ability to refuse to use generative AI when a developer doesn't feel like it's the right choice for the job.

According to a Kotaku report, these efforts to unionize the Magic Arena team began several years ago, after Hasbro laid off about 2,000 employees in 2023. The company also laid off an additional number of employees last year.  

They're also looking for protections over mandatory crunch time, as well as additional protections around clear workforce expectations, defined job roles, and career progression paths. Another concern is over employees’ own personal projects, which Hasbro can claim ownership over, even if made in an employee’s free time with their own resources.

“At Wizards, we’re organizing for a say in layoffs, accountability that runs up and down the chain, and a living wage that actually lets people build a life,” Senior Software Development Engineer and UWOTC-CWA member Damien Wilson lays out. 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/magic-the-gathering-arena-devs-to-form-union-first-under-wizards-of-the-coast-2000137926
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: FFXIV's Critically-Acclaimed Shadowbringers Is Free Now As Part Of Extended Trial
item.content:encoded:
Do you know that one expansion within the critically-acclaimed MMORPG Final Fantasy 14 that’s especially critically-acclaimed, and that everyone says is “worth the effort?” Now, you can play that entirely for free. 

As of the patch that arrived today in Final Fantasy 14, the Shadowbringers expansion is now included in the extended free trial which, as you may have infamously heard by now, has no limits on playtime. This inclusion also brings the free level cap up to 80 and allows players to make female Viera characters and play as Dancer or Gunbreaker. 

The story of the expansion gets pretty spoilery fast, but in short, the Warrior of Light is rebranded to the Warrior of Darkness as they’re called to another world in order to save it from the light. Touting a well-praised story well into even the follow-up patches, it features characters and designs that have gone on to become beloved in the community, and even highly desirable parts of the Magic: The Gathering’s popular Final Fantasy collaboration,  

Shadowbringers has a lot of callbacks to Final Fantasy 3, features optional boss fights with the Ruby Weapon, Emerald Weapon and Diamond Weapon from Final Fantasy 7, as well as a NieR franchise crossover questline with 24-player raids. 

If this arrival seems sudden, that’s because the Shadowbringers announcement for this patch only came this past Friday during the lengthy Keynote presentation during the FFXIV Fan Festival in Anaheim, which also went over much of what’s to come during the rest of the year’s Dawntrail patches. We knew just about everything else about Patch, which arrived today with new main story content, a 24-player raid, and more. 

Also announced last week were the Switch 2 port arriving this August, with a free playtest the month prior, as well as the next expansion, Evercold, arriving in January 2027 and some serious heavy-hitting combat changes alongside it.

Lastly, this announcement came with stunning new official art: 



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/ffxivs-critically-acclaimed-shadowbringers-is-free-now-as-part-of-extended-trial-2000137925
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
